world.title: Africa: China's Africa Strategy Is Shifting and Iran Conflict Will Speed It Up
world.description: Analysis - The global geoeconomic volatility wrought by the second Donald Trump US presidency and hostilities in the Middle East make the shift in China's Africa strategy even more important for China and for Africa.

world.description: In a Q&A with Freedom House, Abdulhakim Idris, head of the Center for Uyghur Studies, discusses his work exposing the Chinese Communist Party’s repression and how Chinese authorities have menaced him and his family in an attempt to prevent him from speaking out.

world.title: Pupil (14) kills nine in Turkey’s second school shooting in two days
world.description: A 14-year-old student shot at least nine people dead, including eight of his fellow pupils and wounded 13 others at a middle school in south-eastern Turkey on Wednesday, officials said, in the country’s second school shooting in two days.

science.title: Scientists discover gene that helps the brain repair itself
science.description: A surprising discovery from high-altitude animals like yaks and Tibetan antelopes could reshape how we treat nerve damage in humans. Scientists found that a genetic mutation helping these animals survive low-oxygen environments also protects and repairs the myelin sheath—the vital coating around nerve fibers that’s damaged in diseases like multiple sclerosis (MS) and cerebral paralysis.

science.title: Scientists discover gene that helps the brain repair itself
science.description: A surprising discovery from high-altitude animals like yaks and Tibetan antelopes could reshape how we treat nerve damage in humans. Scientists found that a genetic mutation helping these animals survive low-oxygen environments also protects and repairs the myelin sheath—the vital coating around nerve fibers that’s damaged in diseases like multiple sclerosis (MS) and cerebral paralysis.

science.title: MIT study reveals a new role for cell membranes
science.description: MIT chemists have found that changing the composition of the cell membrane can alter the function of EGFR, a cell receptor that promotes proliferation and is often overactive in cancer cells.

science.title: Waves hit different on other planets
science.description: From lazy ripples to towering breakers, the mechanics of ocean waves should vary widely from one planet to another, according to a model developed by scientists at MIT and the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institute.

ai.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI shifts its focus to business users amid Anthropic pressure

ai.title: Irish firm Spanish Point Technologies helping to save musicians from being ripped off by AI fraudsters
ai.description: An Irish company has this week launched a new AI product that aims to protect €1.2bn in music royalties for bands and record labels that are under attack from pirates, copyright thieves and AI music knock-off merchants who upload tracks to platforms such as Spotify, deploy bot farms to click on the tracks millions of times and then try to get paid.

ai.title: LinkedIn data records a 20% fall in hiring, finds no AI impact yet
ai.description: Hiring may be down, but AI is not the villain just yet. LinkedIn’s data suggests economic pressures, not automation, are driving the slowdown. Still, the ground is shifting beneath workers’ feet as skills evolve rapidly, signalling a future where jobs may not disappear, but will look very different.

ai.title: Thinking Of Buying An AI Toy For Your Kid? Read This First
ai.description: AI-powered toys are under scrutiny after US senators Richard Blumenthal and Marsha Blackburn warned they may expose children to explicit content, safety risks, and data privacy concerns. Here's how they can impact your children., Technology & Science, Times Now
